create:
✨Create a resource from a file or from stdin
JSON and YAML formats are accepted.
e.g. create -f ./pod.json                    --- Create a pod using the data in pod.json
     create -f registry.yaml --edit -o json  --- Edit the data in registry.yaml in JSON then create the resource using the edited data
create clusterrolebinding: ✨Create a cluster role binding for a particular cluster role
create configmap: ✨Create a config map from a local file, directory or literal value
create cronjob: ✨Create a cron job with the specified name
create deployment: ✨Create a deployment with the specified name
create ingress: ✨Create an ingress with the specified name
create job: ✨Create a job with the specified name
create namespace: ✨Create a namespace with the specified name
create poddisruptionbudget: ✨Create a pod disruption budget with the specified name
create priorityclass: ✨Create a priority class with the specified name
create quota: ✨Create a quota with the specified name
create rolebinding: ✨Create a role binding for a particular role or cluster role
create secret: ✨Create a secret using a specified subcommand
create service: ✨Create a service using a specified subcommand
create serviceaccount: ✨Create a service account with the specified name
create token: ✨Create a service account with the specified name
expose: ✨Take a replication controller, service, deployment or pod and expose it as a new Kubernetes service
run: ✨Run a particular image on the cluster
set env: ✨Update environment variables on a pod template
set image: ✨Update the image of a pod template
set resources: ✨Update resource requests/limits on objects with pod templates
set serviceaccount: ✨Update the service account of a resource
set subject: ✨Update the user, group, or service account in a role binding or cluster role binding
delete: ✨Delete resources by file names, stdin, resources and names, or by resources and label selector
scale: ✨Set a new size for a deployment, replica set, or replication controller
autoscale: ✨Auto-scale a deployment, replica set, stateful set, or replication controller
cluster-info dump: ✨Dump relevant information for debugging and diagnosis
cordon:
✨Mark node as unschedulable
e.g. kubectl cordon foo   --- Mark node "foo" as unschedulable
uncordon:
✨Mark node as schedulable
e.g. kubectl uncordon foo   --- Mark node "foo" as schedulable
taint: ✨Update the taints on one or more nodes
describe: ✨Show details of a specific resource or group of resources
logs: ✨Print the logs for a container in a pod
port-forward: ✨Forward one or more local ports to a pod
proxy: ✨Run a proxy to the Kubernetes API server
cp: ✨Copy files and directories to and from containers
auth reconcile: ✨Reconciles rules for RBAC role, role binding, cluster role, and cluster role binding objects
debug: ✨Create debugging sessions for troubleshooting workloads and nodes
diff: ✨Diff the live version against a would-be applied version
apply: ✨Apply a configuration to a resource by file name or stdin
replace: ✨Replace a resource by file name or stdin
wait: ✨Experimental: Wait for a specific condition on one or many resources
kustomize: ✨Build a kustomization target from a directory or URL
label: ✨Update the labels on a resource
completion: ✨Output shell completion code for the specified shell (bash, zsh, fish, or powershell)
api-resources: ✨Print the supported API resources on the server
api-versions: ✨Print the supported API versions on the server, in the form of "group/version"
config delete-cluster: ✨Delete the specified cluster from the kubeconfig
config delete-context: ✨Delete the specified context from the kubeconfig
config delete-user: ✨Delete the specified user from the kubeconfig
config rename-context: ✨Rename a context from the kubeconfig file
config set: ✨Set an individual value in a kubeconfig file
config unset: ✨Unset an individual value in a kubeconfig file
config use-context: ✨Set the current-context in a kubeconfig file
config view: ✨Display merged kubeconfig settings or a specified kubeconfig file
plugin list: ✨List all visible plugin executables on a user's PATH
version: ✨Print the client and server version information
options: ✨List some options can be passed to any command

kubectl_core_info.__allow_missing_template_keys:
✨If true, ignore any errors in templates when a field or map key is missing in the template.
Only applies to golang and jsonpath output formats.
kubectl_core_info.__dry_run:
✨Must be "none", "server", or "client".
If client strategy, only print the object that would be sent, without sending it.
If server strategy, submit server-side request without persisting the resource.
kubectl_core_info.__field_manager: ✨Name of the manager used to track field ownership.
kubectl_core_info.__filename:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files to use to {{$commandAst.CommandElements[-1]}} the resource
kubectl_core_info.__kustomize:
Alias: --kustomize=''
✨Process the kustomization directory. This flag can't be used together with -f or -R.
kubectl_core_info.__output:
Alias: --output=''
✨Output format. 
One of: (json, yaml, name, go-template, go-template-file, template, templatefile, jsonpath,jsonpath-as-json, jsonpath-file).
kubectl_core_info.__raw:
✨Raw URI to POST to the server.
Uses the transport specified by the kubeconfig file.
kubectl_core_info.__recursive:
Alias: --recursive=false
✨Process the directory used in -f, --filename recursively.
Useful when you want to manage related manifests organized within the same directory.
kubectl_core_info.__save_config:
✨If true, the configuration of current object will be saved in its annotation.
Otherwise, the annotation will be unchanged.
This flag is useful when you want to perform kubectl apply on this object in the future.
kubectl_core_info.__selector:
Alias: --selector=''
✨Selector (label query) to filter on, supports '=', '==', and '!='.(e.g. -l key1=value1,key2=value2).
Matching objects must satisfy all of the specified label constraints.
kubectl_core_info.__show_managed_fields: ✨If true, keep the managedFields when printing objects in JSON or YAML format.
kubectl_core_info.__template:
✨Template string or path to template file to use when -o=go-template, -o=go-template-file.
The template format is golang templates [http://golang.org/pkg/text/template/#pkg-overview].
kubectl_core_info.__validate:
✨Must be one of: strict (or true), warn, ignore (or false).
"true" or "strict" will use a schema to validate the input and fail the request if invalid.
It will perform server side validation if ServerSideFieldValidation is enabled on the api-server,
but will fall back to less reliable client-side validation if not.
"warn" will warn about unknown or duplicate fields without blocking the request if server-side field validation is enabled on the API server, and behave as "ignore" otherwise.
"false" or "ignore" will not perform any schema validation, silently dropping any unknown or duplicate fields.
kubectl_core_info.__windows_line_endings: ✨Only relevant if --edit=true. Defaults to the line ending native to your platform.
kubectl_core_info.__cluster_ip: ✨ClusterIP to be assigned to the service. Leave empty to auto-allocate, or set to 'None' to create a headless service.
kubectl_core_info.__external_ip:
✨Additional external IP address (not managed by Kubernetes) to accept for the service.
If this IP is routed to a node, the service can be accessed by this IP in addition to its generated service IP.
kubectl_core_info.__labels:
Alias: --labels=''
✨Labels to apply to the service created by this call.
kubectl_core_info.__load_balancer_ip: ✨IP to assign to the LoadBalancer. If empty, an ephemeral IP will be created and used (cloud-provider specific).
kubectl_core_info.__name: ✨The name for the newly created object.
kubectl_core_info.__override_type: ✨The method used to override the generated object: json, merge, or strategic.
kubectl_core_info.__overrides:
✨An inline JSON override for the generated object.
If this is non-empty, it is used to override the generated object.
Requires that the object supply a valid apiVersion field.
kubectl_core_info.__port: ✨The port that the service should serve on. Copied from the resource being exposed, if unspecified
kubectl_core_info.__protocol: ✨The network protocol for the service to be created. Default is 'TCP'.
kubectl_core_info.__selector_e:
✨A label selector to use for this service. Only equality-based selector requirements are supported.
If empty (the default) infer the selector from the replication controller or replica set.)
kubectl_core_info.__session_affinity: ✨If non-empty, set the session affinity for the service to this; legal values: 'None', 'ClientIP'
kubectl_core_info.__target_port: ✨Name or number for the port on the container that the service should direct traffic to. Optional.
kubectl_core_info.__type: ✨Type for this service: ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, or ExternalName. Default is 'ClusterIP'.
kubectl_core_info.__attach:
✨If true, wait for the Pod to start running, and then attach to the Pod as if 'kubectl attach ...' were called.
Default false, unless '-i/--stdin' is set, in which case the default is true.
 With '--restart=Never' the exit code of the container process is returned.
kubectl_core_info.__command:
✨If true and extra arguments are present, use them as the 'command' field in the container,
 rather than the 'args' field which is the default.
kubectl_core_info.__env: ✨Environment variables to set in the container.
kubectl_core_info.__expose:
✨If true, create a ClusterIP service associated with the pod.
Requires '--port'.
kubectl_core_info.__image: ✨The image for the container to run.
kubectl_core_info.__image_pull_policy:
✨The image pull policy for the container.
If left empty, this value will not be specified by the client and defaulted by the server.
kubectl_core_info.__labels_r:
Alias: --labels=''
✨Comma separated labels to apply to the pod. Will override previous values.
kubectl_core_info.__leave_stdin_open:
✨If the pod is started in interactive mode or with stdin, leave stdin open after the first attach completes.
By default, stdin will be closed after the first attach completes.
kubectl_core_info.__pod_running_timeout: ✨The length of time (like 5s, 2m, or 3h, higher than zero) to wait until at least one pod is running
kubectl_core_info.__privileged: ✨If true, run the container in privileged mode.
kubectl_core_info.__restart: ✨The restart policy for this Pod.  Legal values [Always, OnFailure, Never].
kubectl_core_info.__rm: ✨If true, delete the pod after it exits.  Only valid when attaching to the container, e.g. with '--attach' or with '-i/--stdin'.
kubectl_core_info.__stdin:
Alias: --stdin=false
✨Keep stdin open on the container(s) in the pod, even if nothing is attached.
kubectl_core_info.__tty:
Alias: --tty=false
✨Allocate a TTY for the container in the pod.
kubectl_core_info.__output_e: ✨Format in which to render the schema. Valid values are: (plaintext, plaintext-openapiv2).
kubectl_core_info.__recursive_e: ✨When true, print the name of all the fields recursively. Otherwise, print the available fields with their description.
kubectl_core_info.__all_namespaces:
Alias: --all-namespaces=false
✨If present, list the requested object(s) across all namespaces.
Namespace in current context is ignored even if specified with --namespace.
kubectl_core_info.__chunk_size: ✨Return large lists in chunks rather than all at once. Pass 0 to disable. This flag is beta and may change in the future.
kubectl_core_info.__field_selector:
✨Selector (field query) to filter on, supports '=', '==', and '!='.(e.g. --field-selector key1=value1,key2=value2).
The server only supports a limited number of field queries per type.
kubectl_core_info.__ignore_not_found: ✨If the requested object does not exist the command will return exit code 0.
kubectl_core_info.__label_columns:
Alias: --label-columns=[]
✨Accepts a comma separated list of labels that are going to be presented as columns. Names are case-sensitive.
You can also use multiple flag options like -L label1 -L label2...
kubectl_core_info.__no_headers: ✨When using the default or custom-column output format, don't print headers (default print headers).
kubectl_core_info.__output_g:
Alias: --output=''
✨Output format.
One of: (json, yaml, name, go-template, go-template-file, template, templatefile, jsonpath,jsonpath-as-json, jsonpath-file, custom-columns, custom-columns-file, wide).
See custom columns [https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/#custom-columns]
golang template [http://golang.org/pkg/text/template/#pkg-overview]
jsonpath template [https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/jsonpath/].
kubectl_core_info.__output_watch_events:
✨Output watch event objects when --watch or --watch-only is used.
Existing objects are output as initial ADDED events.
kubectl_core_info.__raw_g:
✨Raw URI to request from the server.
Uses the transport specified by the kubeconfig file.
kubectl_core_info.__server_print: ✨If true, have the server return the appropriate table output. Supports extension APIs and CRDs.
kubectl_core_info.__show_kind: ✨If present, list the resource type for the requested object(s).
kubectl_core_info.__show_labels: ✨When printing, show all labels as the last column (default hide labels column)
kubectl_core_info.__sort_by:
✨If non-empty, sort list types using this field specification.
The field specification is expressed as a JSONPath expression (e.g. '{.metadata.name}').
The field in the API resource specified by this JSONPath expression must be an integer or a string.
kubectl_core_info.__subresource:
✨If specified, gets the subresource of the requested object.
Must be one of [status scale]. This flag is beta and may change in the future.
kubectl_core_info.__watch:
Alias: --watch=false
✨After listing/getting the requested object, watch for changes.
kubectl_core_info.__watch_only: ✨Watch for changes to the requested object(s), without listing/getting first.
kubectl_core_info.__output_patch: ✨Output the patch if the resource is edited.
kubectl_core_info.__windows_line_endings_e: ✨Defaults to the line ending native to your platform.
kubectl_core_info.__all: ✨Delete all resources in the namespace of the specified resource types.
kubectl_core_info.__cascade:
✨Must be "background", "orphan", or "foreground".
Selects the deletion cascading strategy for the dependents(e.g. Pods created by a ReplicationController).
Defaults to background.
kubectl_core_info.__force:
✨If true, immediately remove resources from API and bypass graceful deletion.
Note that immediate deletion of some resources may result in inconsistency or data loss and requires confirmation.
kubectl_core_info.__grace_period:
✨Period of time in seconds given to the resource to terminate gracefully. Ignored if negative.
Set to 1 for immediate shutdown. Can only be set to 0 when --force is true (force deletion).
kubectl_core_info.__ignore_not_found_d: ✨Treat "resource not found" as a successful delete. Defaults to "true" when --all is specified.
kubectl_core_info.__now: ✨If true, resources are signaled for immediate shutdown (same as --grace-period=1).
kubectl_core_info.__output_d:
Alias: --output=''
✨Output mode. Use "-o name" for shorter output (resource/name).
kubectl_core_info.__raw_d: ✨Raw URI to DELETE to the server.  Uses the transport specified by the kubeconfig file.
kubectl_core_info.__timeout: ✨The length of time to wait before giving up on a delete, zero means determine a timeout from the size of the object
kubectl_core_info.__wait: ✨If true, wait for resources to be gone before returning. This waits for finalizers.
kubectl_core_info.__all_s: ✨Select all resources in the namespace of the specified resource types
kubectl_core_info.__current_replicas: ✨Precondition for current size. Requires that the current size of the resource match this value in order to scale. -1 (default) for no condition.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_s:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files identifying the resource to set a new size
kubectl_core_info.__replicas: ✨The new desired number of replicas. Required.
kubectl_core_info.__resource_version: ✨Precondition for resource version. Requires that the current resource version match this value in order to scale.
kubectl_core_info.__timeout_s:
✨The length of time to wait before giving up on a scale operation, zero means don't wait.
Any other values should contain a corresponding time unit (e.g. 1s, 2m, 3h).
kubectl_core_info.__cpu_percent:
✨The target average CPU utilization (represented as a percent of requested CPU) over all the pods.
If it's not specified or negative, a default autoscaling policy will be used.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_a:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files identifying the resource to autoscale.
kubectl_core_info.__max: ✨The upper limit for the number of pods that can be set by the autoscaler. Required.
kubectl_core_info.__min:
✨The lower limit for the number of pods that can be set by the autoscaler.
If it's not specified or negative, the server will apply a default value.
kubectl_core_info.__name_a:
✨The name for the newly created object.
If not specified, the name of the input resource will be used.
kubectl_core_info.__delete_emptydir_data: ✨Continue even if there are pods using emptyDir (local data that will be deleted when the node is drained).
kubectl_core_info.__disable_eviction:
✨Force drain to use delete, even if eviction is supported.
This will bypass checking PodDisruptionBudgets, use with caution.
kubectl_core_info.__force_d: ✨Continue even if there are pods that do not declare a controller.
kubectl_core_info.__grace_period_d: ✨Period of time in seconds given to each pod to terminate gracefully. If negative, the default value specified in the pod will be used.
kubectl_core_info.__pod_selector: ✨Label selector to filter pods on the node
kubectl_core_info.__skip_wait_for_delete_timeout:
✨If pod DeletionTimestamp older than N seconds, skip waiting for the pod.
Seconds must be greater than 0 to skip.
kubectl_core_info.__timeout_d: ✨The length of time to wait before giving up, zero means infinite
kubectl_core_info.__overwrite: ✨If true, allow taints to be overwritten, otherwise reject taint updates that overwrite existing taints.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_de:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files containing the resource to describe
kubectl_core_info.__show_events: ✨If true, display events related to the described object.
kubectl_core_info.__all_containers: ✨Get all containers' logs in the pod(s).
kubectl_core_info.__container:
Alias: --container=''
✨Print the logs of this container
kubectl_core_info.__follow:
Alias: --follow=false
✨Specify if the logs should be streamed.
kubectl_core_info.__ignore_errors: ✨If watching / following pod logs, allow for any errors that occur to be non-fatal
kubectl_core_info.__insecure_skip_tls_verify_backend:
✨Skip verifying the identity of the kubelet that logs are requested from.
In theory, an attacker could provide invalid log content back.
You might want to use this if your kubelet serving certificates have expired.
kubectl_core_info.__limit_bytes: ✨Maximum bytes of logs to return. Defaults to no limit.
kubectl_core_info.__max_log_requests:
✨Specify maximum number of concurrent logs to follow when using by a selector.
Defaults to 5.
kubectl_core_info.__prefix: ✨Prefix each log line with the log source (pod name and container name)
kubectl_core_info.__since:
✨Only return logs newer than a relative duration like 5s, 2m, or 3h.
Defaults to all logs.
Only one of since-time / since may be used.
kubectl_core_info.__since_time:
✨Only return logs after a specific date (RFC3339).
Defaults to all logs.
Only one of since-time / since may be used.
kubectl_core_info.__tail:
✨Lines of recent log file to display.
Defaults to -1 with no selector, showing all log lines otherwise 10, if a selector is provided.
kubectl_core_info.__timestamps: ✨Include timestamps on each line in the log output
kubectl_core_info.__container_a:
Alias: --container=''
✨Container name. If omitted, use the kubectl.kubernetes.io/default-container annotation for selecting the container to be attached or the first container in the pod will be chosen
kubectl_core_info.__quiet_a:
Alias: --quiet=false
✨Only print output from the remote session
kubectl_core_info.__filename_e:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨to use to exec into the resource
kubectl_core_info.__address:
✨Addresses to listen on (comma separated). Only accepts IP addresses or localhost as a value.
When localhost is supplied, kubectl will try to bind on both 127.0.0.1 and ::1 and will fail if neither of these addresses are available to bind.
kubectl_core_info.__accept_hosts: ✨Regular expression for hosts that the proxy should accept.
kubectl_core_info.__accept_paths: ✨Regular expression for paths that the proxy should accept.
kubectl_core_info.__address_p: ✨The IP address on which to serve on.
kubectl_core_info.__api_prefix: ✨Prefix to serve the proxied API under.
kubectl_core_info.__append_server_path: ✨If true, enables automatic path appending of the kube context server path to each request.
kubectl_core_info.__disable_filter: ✨If true, disable request filtering in the proxy. This is dangerous, and can leave you vulnerable to XSRF attacks, when used with an accessible port.
kubectl_core_info.__keepalive: ✨keepalive specifies the keep-alive period for an active network connection. Set to 0 to disable keepalive.
kubectl_core_info.__port_p:
Alias: --port=8001
✨The port on which to run the proxy. Set to 0 to pick a random port.
kubectl_core_info.__reject_methods: ✨Regular expression for HTTP methods that the proxy should reject (example --reject-methods='POST,PUT,PATCH').
kubectl_core_info.__reject_paths:
✨Regular expression for paths that the proxy should reject.
Paths specified here will be rejected even accepted by --accept-paths.
kubectl_core_info.__unix_socket:
Alias: --unix-socket=''
✨Unix socket on which to run the proxy.Unix socket on which to run the proxy.
kubectl_core_info.__www:
Alias: --www=''
✨Also serve static files from the given directory under the specified prefix.
kubectl_core_info.__www_prefix:
Alias: --www-prefix='/static/'
✨Prefix to serve static files under, if static file directory is specified.
kubectl_core_info.__no_preserve: ✨The copied file/directory's ownership and permissions will not be preserved in the container
kubectl_core_info.__retries:
✨Set number of retries to complete a copy operation from a container.
Specify 0 to disable or any negative value for infinite retrying. The default is 0 (no retry).
kubectl_core_info.__arguments_only: ✨If specified, everything after -- will be passed to the new container as Args instead of Command.
kubectl_core_info.__attach_d:
✨If true, wait for the container to start running, and then attach as if 'kubectl attach ...' were called.
Default false, unless '-i/--stdin' is set, in which case the default is true.
kubectl_core_info.__container_d:
Alias: --container=''
✨Container name to use for debug container.
kubectl_core_info.__copy_to: ✨Create a copy of the target Pod with this name.
kubectl_core_info.__image_d: ✨Container image to use for debug container.
kubectl_core_info.__replace: ✨When used with '--copy-to', delete the original Pod.
kubectl_core_info.__same_node: ✨When used with '--copy-to', schedule the copy of target Pod on the same node.
kubectl_core_info.__set_image: ✨When used with '--copy-to', a list of name=image pairs for changing container images, similar to how 'kubectl set image' works.
kubectl_core_info.__share_processes: ✨When used with '--copy-to', enable process namespace sharing in the copy.
kubectl_core_info.__target: ✨When using an ephemeral container, target processes in this container name.
kubectl_core_info.__tty_d:
Alias: --tty=false
✨Allocate a TTY for the debugging container.
kubectl_core_info.__for: ✨Filter events to only those pertaining to the specified resource.
kubectl_core_info.__watch_e:
Alias: --watch=false
✨After listing the requested events, watch for more events.
kubectl_core_info.__concurrency:
✨Number of objects to process in parallel when diffing against the live version.
Larger number = faster, but more memory, I/O and CPU over that shorter period of time.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_diff:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files contains the configuration to diff
kubectl_core_info.__force_conflicts: ✨If true, server-side apply will force the changes against conflicts.
kubectl_core_info.__prune: ✨Include resources that would be deleted by pruning. Can be used with -l and default shows all resources would be pruned
kubectl_core_info.__server_side: ✨If true, apply runs in the server instead of the client.
kubectl_core_info.__show_managed_fields_d: ✨If true, include managed fields in the diff.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_apply:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨The files that contain the configurations to {{$commandAst.CommandElements[-1]}}.
kubectl_core_info.__openapi_patch:
✨If true, use openapi to calculate diff when the openapi presents and the resource can be found in the openapi spec.
Otherwise, fall back to use baked-in types.
kubectl_core_info.__overwrite_a: ✨Automatically resolve conflicts between the modified and live configuration by using values from the modified configuration
kubectl_core_info.__prune_a: ✨Automatically delete resource objects, that do not appear in the configs and are created by either apply or create --save-config. Should be used with either -l or --all.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_p:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files identifying the resource to update
kubectl_core_info.__local: ✨If true, patch will operate on the content of the file, not the server-side resource.
kubectl_core_info.__patch:
Alias: --patch=''
✨The patch to be applied to the resource JSON file.
kubectl_core_info.__patch_file: ✨A file containing a patch to be applied to the resource.
kubectl_core_info.__subresource_p:
✨ If specified, patch will operate on the subresource of the requested object.
Must be one of [status scale].
This flag is beta and may change in the future.
kubectl_core_info.__type_p: ✨The type of patch being provided; one of [json merge strategic]
kubectl_core_info.__raw_r: ✨Raw URI to PUT to the server.  Uses the transport specified by the kubeconfig file.
kubectl_core_info.__for_w:
✨The condition to wait on: 
[delete|condition=condition-name[=condition-value]|jsonpath='{JSONPath expression}'=[JSONPath value]]. 
The default condition-value is true.
Condition values are compared afterUnicode simple case folding, which is a more general form of case-insensitivity.
kubectl_core_info.__local_w: ✨If true, annotation will NOT contact api-server but run locally.
kubectl_core_info.__selector_w:
Alias: --selector=''
✨Selector (label query) to filter on, supports '=', '==', and '!='.(e.g. -l key1=value1,key2=value2)
kubectl_core_info.__timeout_w:
✨The length of time to wait before giving up.
Zero means check once and don't wait, negative means wait for a week.
kubectl_core_info.__as_current_user: ✨use the uid and gid of the command executor to run the function in the container
kubectl_core_info.__env_k:
Alias: --env=[]
✨a list of environment variables to be used by functions
kubectl_core_info.__load_restrictor:
✨if set to 'LoadRestrictionsNone', local kustomizations may load files from outside their root.
This does,however, break the relocatability of the kustomization.
kubectl_core_info.__mount: ✨a list of storage options read from the filesystem
kubectl_core_info.__network: ✨enable network access for functions that declare it
kubectl_core_info.__network_name: ✨the docker network to run the container in
kubectl_core_info.__output_k:
Alias: --output=''
✨If specified, write output to this path.
kubectl_core_info.__all_namespaces_l:
Alias: --all-namespaces=false
✨If true, check the specified action in all namespaces
kubectl_core_info.__filename_l:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files identifying the resource to update the labels
kubectl_core_info.__list: ✨If true, display the labels for a given resource.
kubectl_core_info.__local_l: ✨If true, label will NOT contact api-server but run locally.
kubectl_core_info.__overwrite_l: ✨If true, allow labels to be overwritten, otherwise reject label updates that overwrite existing labels.
kubectl_core_info.__resource_version_l:
✨If non-empty, the labels update will only succeed if this is the current resource-version for the object.
Only valid when specifying a single resource.
kubectl_core_info.__filename_an:
Alias: --filename=[]
✨Filename, directory, or URL to files identifying the resource to update the annotation
kubectl_core_info.__list_a: ✨If true, display the annotations for a given resource.
kubectl_core_info.__local_a: ✨If true, annotation will NOT contact api-server but run locally.
kubectl_core_info.__overwrite_an: ✨If true, allow annotations to be overwritten, otherwise reject annotation updates that overwrite existing annotations.
kubectl_core_info.__resource_version_a:
✨If non-empty, the annotation update will only succeed if this is the current resource-version for the object.
Only valid when specifying a single resource.
kubectl_core_info.__api_group: ✨Limit to resources in the specified API group.
kubectl_core_info.__cached: ✨Use the cached list of resources if available.
kubectl_core_info.__categories: ✨Limit to resources that belong to the specified categories.
kubectl_core_info.__namespaced: ✨If false, non-namespaced resources will be returned, otherwise returning namespaced resources by default.
kubectl_core_info.__sort_by_a: ✨If non-empty, sort list of resources using specified field. The field can be either 'name' or 'kind'.
kubectl_core_info.__verbs: ✨Limit to resources that support the specified verbs.
kubectl_core_info.__client: ✨If true, shows client version only (no server required).

common_options.--as='': ✨Username to impersonate for the operation. User could be a regular user or a service account in a namespace.
common_options.--as-group=[]: ✨Group to impersonate for the operation, this flag can be repeated to specify multiple groups.
common_options.--certificate-authority='': ✨Path to a cert file for the certificate authority
common_options.--client-certificate='': ✨Path to a client certificate file for TLS
common_options.--client-key='': ✨Path to a client key file for TLS
common_options.--cluster='': ✨The name of the kubeconfig cluster to use
common_options.--context='': ✨The name of the kubeconfig context to use
common_options.--disable-compression=false: ✨If true, opt-out of response compression for all requests to the server
common_options.--insecure-skip-tls-verify=false: ✨If true, the server's certificate will not be checked for validity. This will make your HTTPS connections insecure
common_options.--kubeconfig='': ✨Path to the kubeconfig file to use for CLI requests
common_options.--log-flush-frequency=5s: ✨Maximum number of seconds between log flushes
common_options.--match-server-version=false: ✨Require server version to match client version
-n: ✨If present, the namespace scope for this CLI request
--namespace='': ✨If present, the namespace scope for this CLI request
common_options.--password='': ✨Password for basic authentication to the API server
common_options.--profile='none': ✨Name of profile to capture. One of (none|cpu|heap|goroutine|threadcreate|block|mutex)
common_options.--profile-output='profile.pprof': ✨Name of the file to write the profile to
--request-timeout='0': ✨The length of time to wait before giving up on a single server request.
Non-zero values should contain a corresponding time unit (e.g. 1s, 2m, 3h). A value of zero means don't timeout requests.
-s: ✨The address and port of the Kubernetes API server
--server='': ✨The address and port of the Kubernetes API server
common_options.--tls-server-name='': ✨ Server name to use for server certificate validation. If it is not provided, the hostname used to contact the server is used
common_options.--token='': ✨Bearer token for authentication to the API server
common_options.--user='': ✨The name of the kubeconfig user to use
common_options.--username='': ✨Username for basic authentication to the API server
common_options.--vmodule=: ✨comma-separated list of pattern=N settings for file-filtered logging (only works for the default text log format)
common_options.--warnings-as-errors=false: ✨Treat warnings received from the server as errors and exit with a non-zero exit code
